articles.summary: Delve ran 4 placements across 2 TLDR newsletters and brought in 66 leads, with a meaningful chunk from enterprise. The result: $1M in attributed pipeline and a 52x ROI.

articles.title: TESLA TARGETS JUNE 12 LAUNCH OF ROBOTAXI SERVICE IN AUSTIN
articles.summary: Tesla plans to launch its long-awaited robotaxi service in Austin on June 12. The date has only been discussed internally and could still change. The company this week operated a test vehicle on public roads in Austin with no one in the driver's seat for the first time. Elon Musk said earlier this month that Tesla would officially roll out a fleet of about 10 self-driving robotaxis in Austin before expanding to a thousand vehicles within a few months.

articles.title: PERPLEXITY'S NEW TOOL CAN GENERATE SPREADSHEETS, DASHBOARDS, AND MORE
articles.summary: Perplexity Labs, a tool now available to Perplexity Pro users, can craft reports, spreadsheets, dashboards, and more. It is available on the web, iOS, and Android and will soon be available on Perplexity's apps for Mac and Windows. The tool is designed to help people complete a variety of work and personal projects. It can take 10 minutes or more to run and use tools like web search, code execution, and chart and image creation.

articles.title: CHINA TO VISIT EARTH'S ‘QUASI-MOON' AND BRING A CHUNK OF IT BACK HOME
articles.summary: China's National Space Agency has launched an asteroid sample mission aimed at Asteroid 2016HO3, aka 469219 Kamoʻoalewa, which orbits the Sun. The Tianwen 2 probe rode a Long March 3B rocket into orbit at 1:31 Thursday morning. 2016HO3 is estimated to have a diameter of 40 to 100 meters. It is an attractive target for exploration because it can be reached by a spacecraft traveling at a modest 7 kilometers per second. The probe will reach the asteroid in about a year and then orbit it for a while before landing and attempting to retrieve around 100 grams of material.

articles.title: HUGGING FACE UNVEILS TWO NEW HUMANOID ROBOTS
articles.summary: Hugging Face has announced a pair of open source humanoid robots. HopeJR is a full-sized humanoid robot with 66 actuated degrees of freedom. It can walk and move its arms. Reachy Mini is a desktop unit that can move its head, talk, listen, and be used to test AI apps. There is no timeline for shipping these robots, but the company anticipates shipping the first few units by the end of the year. The waitlist is currently open. A video of HopeJR being demonstrated at an event is available in the article.

articles.title: WHAT'S LAUNCHING AT PLAID EFFECTS?
articles.summary: Plaid Effects [9] is back on June 12. Tune in for a first look at new products to help you improve credit decisions, stop fraud earlier, and move faster—plus live deep dives with the teams behind the products. Don't miss what's next in financial services.

articles.title: ANNOUNCING TYPESCRIPT NATIVE PREVIEWS
articles.summary: Developers can now use npm to get a preview of the native TypeScript compiler. They can now also use a preview version of TypeScript editor functionality for VS Code through the Visual Studio Marketplace. These previews will eventually become TypeScript 7. They will be published nightly so that developers can easily try the latest developments. The VS Code extension should get automatic updates by default.

articles.title: BUILDING A DISTRIBUTED CACHE FOR S3
articles.summary: Clickhouse built a distributed cache for cloud object storage - a shared, low-latency layer that gives all compute nodes fast access to hot data. This post looks at how hot data caching worked before, why object storage made it hard, and how the new architecture fixes it. It includes benchmarks.

articles.title: PATTERN MATCHING 20 HABITS OF EXCEPTIONAL STARTUPS
articles.summary: Exceptional startups do things very differently from the median startup. This post looks at habits that these startups tend to have. Exceptional startups beat and raise expectations, recruit top talent obsessively, ship product incredibly fast, and obsess over product quality. These traits, along with many others, make them great places to work and invest in.

articles.title: KNOWLEDGE WORK IS DYING—HERE'S WHAT COMES NEXT
articles.summary: Before AI, knowledge set people apart - knowing more meant earning more. AI models can swallow entire fields overnight. This makes it more important to have wisdom. This article looks at how to develop abilities in areas that AI can't replicate. It teaches leaders how to lead from a deeper place of wisdom.

articles.summary: We also write TLDR AI. Same format, all about LLMs, GenAI, and deep

articles.title: learning. Delivered every weekday. Subscribe for free.
THE CASE FOR USING A WEB BROWSER AS YOUR TERMINAL
articles.summary: Tweety is an integrated terminal for the browser that minimizes context switching by allowing developers to interact with their terminal directly from their browsers.

articles.title: META AND ANDURIL DEFENSE STARTUP PARTNER ON VR, AR PROJECT INTENDED FOR U.S. ARMY
articles.summary: Meta and Anduril have formed a partnership to create virtual and augmented reality devices intended for use by the US Army.

articles.title: APPLE'S US APP STORE TOPPED $400B IN DEVELOPER BILLINGS AND SALES IN 2024
articles.summary: The figure has nearly tripled in size since 2019.

articles.title: HUMAN CODERS ARE STILL BETTER THAN LLMS
articles.summary: While current-level AI is useful, it is still very much behind human intelligence.

articles.title: ONLOOK
articles.summary: Onlook is a visual-first code editor designed for designers that allows them to craft websites, prototypes, and designs with AI.

articles.title: YOU'LL NEVER BE READY FOR ANYTHING THAT MATTERS
articles.summary: You'll never be ready for the big things in life, so just start doing them anyway.
